best: Asks whether op/txn records can be GC'd before the data they describe, reasoning that without commit/abort info a new reader cannot decide which entries to skip — a non-obvious consumer-consistency dependency.

Questions whether DISABLED_INHERITED, which is only used in a setter, actually expresses 'inherit to children' vs 'has inherited' — a real API-semantics flaw in the new enum, while the rest are Javadoc-wording nits.

Flags Boolean vs boolean and the resulting NPE risk when unboxing a null preventLogging flag; the remaining points are naming/wording preferences (and the reviewer missed the static mutable anonymizerType field).

Catches that defaulting systemTopicDefaultNumPartitions to 1 breaks backward compatibility for existing deployments and proposes a -1 sentinel that falls back to defaultNumPartitions, plus a missing > 0 validation check.
Spots leftover Git merge-conflict markers (<<<<<<< / ======= / >>>>>>>) committed in the file and explains how to resolve them — a build-breaking issue easy to miss in a large doc-only diff.

Argues the SVG-vs-raster stream sniffing (mark/reset or PushbackInputStream) belongs in FileFormat.load rather than ImageLoader/JSVGRasterizer, and separately notices the latest update accidentally dropped the Linux/Mac implementations.
Warns that JSR-330 injection does not work reliably on concrete classes and needs an interface, and that the mojo should take a Repository (not a String URL) so ID-based proxy/auth/mirroring works.

Repeatedly pushes to replace CDI.current().select(OpenTelemetry.class) with a proper Instance<OpenTelemetry> injection point / constructor parameter — a real Quarkus/Arc anti-pattern — and flags a redundant pom dependency.
Pushes back on adding a separate branchy plus branchless code path, arguing the maintainability cost outweighs the gain on the rare many-nulls path; the rest are redundant-else and rename nits.
Raises that applying the new GC.create helper in places with large reformatting will damage git-blame history, which SWT relies on — a non-obvious project-specific maintainability concern.

Identifies a behavioral regression — removing the job-list-add code broke instant job-list updates — and that the catch block rethrows RuntimeException without chaining the original cause, losing the stack trace.
Notes the new CursorHandleProvider inner classes should be static to prevent accidental access to the outer Cursor instance; the remaining points are formatting/ordering/rename style nits.
Flags an unsure >>> vs >> signed/unsigned-shift correctness question in RefCnt; most other points are self-authored leftover/TODO notes rather than independent review findings.

Gives the correct SmallRyeConfigBuilder recipe (setAddDefaultSources(false).addSystemSources().withSources(inFileSystem...)) and points out the builder's own classloader must be used — concrete correctness fixes for the native config builder.
